reasoning: The Lean formalization has two serious semantic mismatches. First, in the informal statement P(x)=ax⁴+bx³+cx²+dx+e, so "b" denotes the coefficient of x³. In Mathlib, `P.coeff n` is the coefficient of Xⁿ (confirmed in Mathlib/Algebra/Polynomial/Basic.lean line 667: "`coeff p n` ... is the coefficient of `X^n` in `p`"), so `P.coeff 1` is the coefficient of x (i.e. "d" in the informal notation), not "b". The Lean exclusion condition therefore refers to -d/(2a) rather than -b/(2a). Second, and more critically, the informal hypothesis "x₁+x₂ ∈ ℚ\{-b/(2a)}" requires x₁+x₂ to be rational AND distinct from the special value, but the Lean hypothesis only excludes the special value — it omits the rationality of x₁+x₂ entirely. Without rationality of the sum, the conclusion fails (e.g., for P(x)=x⁴-2, take x₁=⁴√2 and x₂=i⁴√2: their sum is not rational, and x₁x₂=i√2 is not rational). So the Lean version is weaker in this critical hypothesis but also mismatches the excluded value, making it not logically comparable to the informal statement.
